reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 3 gates passed. Gate 1 (Dead Code): Function is actively called by 7+ language bindings (Go, Java, Python, Rust, Node.js, Nim, Elixir). Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): Library processes UNTRUSTED P2P peer data; not a trusted source scenario. Gate 3 (Scope): EIP-7594 PeerDAS is in-scope for Fusaka. DOWNGRADED from HIGH to MEDIUM because Phase 03 confirmed all major language bindings (Go, Node.js, Python, Java, Rust) validate array lengths before calling C function. Vulnerability requires direct C/C++ caller or incorrect FFI usage, limiting network impact to <33% (likely only affects clients using unvalidated direct C bindings, which are not the mainstream implementations).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: EIP-7594 PeerDAS - verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch must validate array length consistency
